serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 250 g, representing a single-plate restaurant portion of mixed melon salad as typically served at EC Coffee Bar.

nutrient_source.title: Cantaloupe, raw (USDA FoodData Central FDC ID 169092) and Honeydew melon, raw (FDC ID 169911); composite estimate for mixed melon salad
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service, FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct entry for Melon Harmony Salad in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA branded foods. Nutrient values estimated as a weighted composite of cantaloupe (50%), honeydew melon (30%), and watermelon (20%) per 100 g edible portion, based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries. Light dressing contribution assumed minimal and not separately itemised. Values represent the fruit-dominant base of the salad.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC; cantaloupe GI = 65
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Melon Harmony Salad. GI of 65 applied based on cantaloupe (GI 65, glucose reference) as the dominant ingredient per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 21.25) / 100 = 13.8, rounded to 13.9. Serving carbohydrate = 8.5 g per 100 g × 250 g serving = 21.25 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Low sodium content with beneficial potassium
hypertension.guidance: Melon Harmony Salad contains approximately 16 mg sodium and 228 mg potassium per 100 g, making it a favourable choice for hypertensive individuals as the high potassium-to-sodium ratio supports blood pressure regulation; however, patients should confirm that any added dressing does not significantly increase sodium load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index and natural fruit sugars
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a GI of approximately 65 (medium) and a glycemic load of around 13.9 per 250 g serving, individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume this salad in controlled portions and pair it with a protein or fibre source to moderate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Very low saturated fat and cholesterol with antioxidant vitamins
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Melon Harmony Salad is very low in saturated fat (0.05 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (0 mg), and provides vitamin C (18 mg) and beta-carotene-derived vitamin A (169 mcg RAE), which support endothelial health; it is a cardioprotective food choice when consumed without high-fat dressings.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Melon Harmony Salad is a fresh fruit salad dish served at EC Coffee Bar on Carpenter Street in Singapore, featuring a combination of melon varieties — typically cantaloupe, honeydew, and watermelon — arranged or tossed together, sometimes with mixed greens and a light dressing. The dish is minimally processed, relying on the natural sweetness, juicy texture, and vibrant colour of fresh melons. The flavour profile is refreshing, mildly sweet, and hydrating, with a soft yet slightly firm texture from the melon flesh. Nutritionally, the salad is low in energy (approximately 36 kcal per 100 g), very low in fat and sodium, and provides a moderate amount of natural sugars (7.8 g per 100 g), dietary fibre (0.7 g), vitamin C (18 mg), and beta-carotene-rich vitamin A (169 mcg RAE). The glycemic index is approximately 65 (medium), driven primarily by cantaloupe, making portion control advisable for individuals managing blood glucose. The dish is suitable for vegetarian, vegan, halal, gluten-free, and dairy-free diets. It is priced at SGD 26.30 at EC Coffee Bar, positioning it as a premium café salad offering. Clinically, it is a low-sodium, potassium-rich, antioxidant-containing dish that supports cardiovascular and blood pressure health, though its moderate GI warrants mindful consumption by diabetic patients.
common_user_queries: Is Melon Harmony Salad good for diabetics?
Melon salad at EC Coffee Bar Singapore

confidence_reason: No direct entry for Melon Harmony Salad exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, IMR Malaysia MyFCD, or USDA FoodData Central branded foods. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted composite of USDA SR Legacy entries for cantaloupe, honeydew, and watermelon. GI is estimated from cantaloupe as the dominant ingredient using the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of a direct dish-level measurement.
notes: Nutrient values are composite estimates based on USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for cantaloupe (FDC 169092), honeydew melon (FDC 169911), and watermelon (FDC 167765), weighted approximately 50/30/20. trans_fat_g is 0.0 as melons contain no trans fats (naturally zero). vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 as plant-based fruit foods contain no vitamin B12 (naturally zero). vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0 as fresh melon contains no meaningful vitamin D (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central). cholesterol_mg is 0.0 as this is a plant-based food (naturally zero). monounsaturated_fat_g (-1 sentinel not used; value estimated from USDA data). All -1 values in per_100g represent nutrients not individually reported in the composite USDA source entries used for estimation. Specifically: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy data and provided as positive numeric values rather than -1 where USDA data was available. No -1 sentinel values remain in per_100g as all fields were estimable from USDA sources. GI set to 65 based on cantaloupe as dominant ingredient per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al. 2008). Serving size of 250 g is an estimate for a restaurant plate portion; actual restaurant portion may vary. Price SGD 26.30 preserved from input source (field survey data, EC Coffee Bar - Carpenter Street). Food is classified under Malaysian cuisine per input, consistent with the restaurant's Malaysian-influenced café menu.

Iteration 1: Added all mandatory multilingual names for Malaysian cuisine (Chinese simplified/traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

Iteration 1: Documented all zero values with justification phrases (naturally zero for trans fat, B12, vitamin D, cholesterol).
Iteration 1: Set ontology match types to exact allowed values only (closest_parent or none).

Iteration 2: Verified GI category threshold — GI 65 is correctly medium (56-69 range). Confirmed no -1 sentinel values remain undocumented. Confirmed all aliases_by_language strings present verbatim in flat aliases.
